approach: A — Conservative: make the exact minimum changes specified in the task description, plus fix the test that directly tests the removed `new Audio()` behaviour so the suite passes

steps.action: Change minWidth/minHeight from 80 to 88 in the outer container style object (line 30)
Add `import { audioManager } from '@/lib/audio';` at the top of the file
In setupRound callback, replace `const audio = new Audio(target.audioEnPath); audio.play().catch(() => {});` with `audioManager.playWordEn(target.audioEnPath);`
Replace Play Again button onClick from `const a = new Audio(current?.audioEnPath); a.play().catch(() => {});` to `if (current?.audioEnPath) audioManager.playWordEn(current.audioEnPath);`
Add `const timeoutsRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>[]>([]);` near existing refs (after wrongAttemptsRef / correctFirstRef)
In handleTap, wrap all three setTimeout calls (1200ms advance, 400ms wrong-card reset, 2000ms reveal-then-advance) to capture return value into `tid` and push onto `timeoutsRef.current`
Add `useEffect` to the import line (useState, useRef, useCallback are already imported)
Add `const timeoutsRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>[]>([]);` near existing refs (after wrongFlipsRef)
In handleFlip, capture the outer 400ms setTimeout return value into `const tid1`; capture the inner 800ms setTimeout return value into `const tid2`; push both onto `timeoutsRef.current`

Replace the test 'plays audio on mount with first word audioEnPath' to mock `audioManager` from '@/lib/audio' and verify `mockPlayWordEn` is called with the first word's audioEnPath instead of asserting on `global.Audio`
Add a test verifying the Play Again button calls audioManager.playWordEn with current word's audioEnPath

expected_tests: Existing: ListenAndFind shows 3 choice cards for age=3
Existing: ListenAndFind shows 4 choice cards for age=4
Updated: ListenAndFind plays audio on mount via audioManager.playWordEn (was: global.Audio)
New: ListenAndFind Play Again button calls audioManager.playWordEn
Existing: MatchingPairs all existing tests pass unchanged
risk_areas: The existing test 'plays audio on mount with first word audioEnPath' asserts on `global.Audio` constructor — it WILL fail after the change. Must update this test.
MatchingPairs does not yet import useEffect — need to add it to the import statement.

The inner 800ms setTimeout in MatchingPairs is nested inside the outer 400ms callback. The inner `tid` capture must be done inside the outer callback before returning, so it is accessible and pushed onto timeoutsRef.
persistence_strategy: N/A — pure UI component changes, no data storage involved
error_handling_approach: audioManager.playWordEn already has internal try/catch with console.warn. Timeout cleanup prevents spurious React state-update-on-unmounted-component errors.
